title: 4chan
description: Newest threads from selected worksafe 4chan boards (news, g, o, tv, v, mu, vip) via the read-only JSON API, plus the official 4chan blog. Board posts are user-generated and unmoderated; each title is prefixed with its board.

items.title: /g/ Technology: Why isn't Windows being decompiled so it's less shitty?
items.content_text: We see all this talk of people decompiling Gamecube games and such with Fable and what not, but what about decompiling explorer.exe from Windows 10 LTSC 2021 to make a replacement so that the start menu doesn't have a 1 second delay when opening? I pretty sure it opened instantly in Windows 7. Or how about using Fable on Firefox source code so that Firefox stops leaking memory?

items.title: /o/ Auto: Has Aprilia left its patchy reliability history in the past?
items.content_text: Ive seen tons of reviewers saying the same thing these days about how aprilia reliability has turned the corner and is now much better than it was 20 yrs ago. Im not going to say its up there with kawasaki suzuki honda and bmw, but i think its good enough for the current reputation of aprilia reliability to be “generally solid”. Ppl have occasional niggles with aprilias here and there, even on the tuareg which might be aprilias most reliable bike ever, but the majority of aprilias that leave the

items.title: /g/ Technology: Is it possible to kind of upgrade the gpu board on Thinkpad P16 Gen 2? (from 3500 ada to 5000 ada)

items.title: /o/ Auto: 1955
items.content_text: It was the only year in American history in which NOBODY made a bad looking automobile. Not even Hudson or DeSoto had a bad outing. It was a hopeful time. More displacement every year. 10 cent gas. Buying a new car every two years. Truly the peak of quality of life for the American working man. But you probably prefer a different year. So what year do you consider peak?

items.title: /mu/ Music: The Life And Times
items.content_text: I think this album holds up, love the off the grid feeling, weird slow parts and dragging rhythms, lots of space between the instruments, neat way some of the guitar lines go with the melodies, great drum parts, distorted bass riffs, just a lot of cool stuff going on I think this album stuck with me more than any of Shiner’s work for some reason https://thelifeandtimes.bandcamp.co m/album/no-one-loves-you-like-i-do

items.title: /mu/ Music: Lily Allen: It's Not Me It's You [Capitol, 2009] She is too a role model--for ch…
items.content_text: Lily Allen: It's Not Me It's You [Capitol, 2009] She is too a role model--for chart queens, bohemians born-and-raised, and paparazzi victims everywhere. True, her synth-pop album debuted below India.Arie at five before its SoundScan swan dive. But even diving she's more graceful than most, and she has every expectation of popping out of the pool and climbing the ladder again, which is how her first one went gold. Here the modestly likable, oddly uncategorizable singer of Alright, Still emerges a

Seriously how do codemonkeys cope with this baffling reality? That such a traditionally creative task as programming got successfully automated, before "boring", "suits", "bullshit jobs", "hr roasties" even? items.title: /o/ Auto: The Last of Old World Luxury
items.content_text: They don't make 'em like they used to... at least not for us proletariat in the entry white-collar class. This isn't an economic discussion. When you get into a new luxury car it feels like tin by comparison, my dad had a 2000 bmw 740iL. I don't claim to know a lot about that car, but when i sat in the backseat I felt like I was in absolute first class. Real wood, leather, that big cabin, instead of some slop tablet new world order eye-tracking camera smartphone-mobile. I bought a volvo xc90 bec

items.title: /v/ Video Games: SNES
items.content_text: Out of every single console in existence, this one is just the best. The controller is amazing. The games are amazing. The graphics are amazing. The sound is amazing. The only issue I have is that it doesn't play NES carts, but it can play gameboy carts with the super gameboy. But still, amazing system. Easily the best.
